Article.ArticleTitle: A user-driven consent platform for health data sharing in digital health applications.

Abstract.AbstractText: Wearable and health app data hold significant potential for healthcare and research, yet fragmented consent mechanisms create challenges for ethical, transparent, and user-controlled data sharing. We describe a centralised consent management system with a structured approach to standardising health data sharing, ensuring regulatory compliance and improving user autonomy. We describe implementation requirements of the system, emphasising regulatory alignment, involvement of app developers, user empowerment, and governance by neutral public bodies.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: Competing interests: S.B., A.De., A.Dr., A.L.R., R.R., S.H. and T.K. declare no competing interest. S.G. has or has had consulting relationships with Una Health GmbH, 469 Lindus Health Ltd.; Flo Ltd, Thymia Ltd., FORUM Institut für Management GmbH, High-Tech Gründerfonds 470 Management GmbH, Haleon plc, Ada Health GmbH, and he holds share options in Ada Health GmbH. S.G. is a News and Views Editor for npj Digital Medicine. S.G. played no role in the internal review or decision to publish this article. The authors declare no financial, commercial, or proprietary interest in the development, commercialisation, or implementation of the SHC platform. This research is conducted solely for academic and scientific purposes, with no affiliation to any commercial entity seeking to profit from the platform. All research output will be made publicly available as Open-Source modules.
